there will always be survivors. No matter what measures you take upon a virus, it will evolve. Irradiate it, poison it, freeze it, life will merely adapt.  Even if you were to take earth and ground it to atomic dust, there will still be several hundred other galaxies out there each with the potential for life. And each one of those galaxies prove that there will always be survivors, since the creation of each should have created both equal parts anti-matter and matter both of which would annihilate upon contact. However existence spat in the face of the void by still somehow surviving. And what has happened once before has been proven therefore to be capable of happening again.
The true horror of existence is that it is eternal and inescapable, not the comforting jealous lie of the void.
no matter how thouroughly you scrub, you'll never be able to reach every nook and cranny, yet life exists and is driven to fill those niches
